title: salix livida var. occidentalis
intro: Salix livida var. occidentalis, also known as western purple willow, is a variety of the Salix livida plant, a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help to reduce pain and swelling. The health benefits of Salix livida var. occidentalis include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of menopausal hot flashes. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, and are often used to treat a range of health issues, including arthritis, digestive problems, and skin conditions.
